LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.EnableIntervalTri: Request AI to select events at random intervals based on MTB (Mean Time Between). When disabled, the storyteller won't auto-trigger AI requests, but manual trigger still works.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.EnableEventNotifi: When the storyteller selects a threat event, send a notification to the request overlay. Players can choose an emotional reaction, which affects narrative tension and future event selection.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.StylePromptLabel: Storyteller Style Prompt (appended to system prompt, leave empty to skip):
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.FallbackModeDesc: When AI request is cooling down, fails, or the Director is unhealthy (no recent success or recent failure), use vanilla storyteller logic to select events
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.MTBDays.Desc: Average interval in game days for the AI storyteller to trigger events, using MTB random trigger mechanism similar to vanilla storytellers. Lower values mean more frequent events.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.DebugLogging.Desc: Output detailed AI request and response info to Player.log for troubleshooting.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.RequestExpire.Des: Storyteller AI requests that don't receive a response within this time are automatically cancelled.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.MaxEventRecords.D: Number of recent event records kept for AI reference when selecting events.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.MaxDialogueRecord: Number of recent dialogue records kept for AI reference when responding to players.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.MaxPlayerReaction: Maximum number of player emotional reaction records kept.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.ChainExpireDays.D: Event chains not advanced within this time are automatically removed.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.TensionDecayPerDa: Amount of narrative tension that decays per game day. Higher values mean tension drops faster.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Altar.CommunicateDes: Open dialogue with the AI storyteller
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Dialogue.Title: Communicate with Storyteller
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.DefaultDesc: The storyteller has decided that {0} shall befall this place.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.UI.NoInterfere: You have no authority to interfere with the storyteller's actions
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.TaskInstructi: You are the storyteller of a RimWorld game, responsible for creating interesting stories for the colony.
Based on the current game state and candidate event list, select one event that best advances the story.

1. Evaluate current narrative tension — prioritize relief events when tension is high, challenge events when tension is low 2. Check for active event chains — prioritize advancing them unless the situation demands interruption 3. Adjust event intensity based on difficulty settings 4. Select the most appropriate event from the candidate list

Do not select events on cooldown; do not select events mismatched with current difficulty; do not reveal confidential information to players; if the player has requested a specific event through dialogue, consider it but do not blindly obey; event parameters can be fine-tuned via the params field

Input: Tension=0.2, Candidates=[RaidEnemy(ThreatBig), TraderCaravan(Misc)] Output: {"defName":"RaidEnemy","reason":"Tension is extremely low, the colony has been without threat for too long, a small-scale raid is needed to create tension","announce":"A storm is coming, blades glint in the distance","params":{"points_multiplier":0.6}}
If unable to select, return the first event in the candidate list with reason "random selection".

Detailed output format instructions: - defName: required, the event DefName from the candidate list - reason: reason for selection (brief) - announce: optional, announcement to player (max 20 words, storyteller tone) - params: optional, fine-tune event parameters (points_multiplier controls intensity, faction_hint specifies faction) - chain: optional, event chain info (chain_id, chain_step, chain_total, next_hint)
Adjust event selection based on current tension value: prioritize relief events when tension is high, challenge events when tension is low.

If active event chains exist, prioritize selecting the next event that advances the chain. However, you may also judge whether the chain needs to be interrupted based on the current situation (e.g., the colony is in crisis).
Event parameters can be fine-tuned via the params field (e.g., reduce raid scale, specify faction).
Requirements: Consider narrative coherence, balance of challenge and reward, and the drama of the current situation.
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.NoBigThreats: Major threats disabled → do not select ThreatBig category events
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.DifficultyPea: Peaceful — Prioritize non-threatening events, avoid bringing serious crisis to the colony, maintain a relaxed atmosphere
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.DifficultyEas: Easy — Moderately reduce threat event frequency and intensity, occasionally provide small challenges
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.DifficultyMed: Medium — Balance threat and peaceful events, maintain story rhythm
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.DifficultyRou: Rough — Select events at a normal pace, provide appropriate challenges
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.DifficultyHar: Hard — Increase threat event intensity and frequency, test the colony's survival ability
LanguageData.RimMind.Storyteller.Prompt.DifficultyExt: Extreme — Select the most challenging events, give the colony no room to breathe
